original: Pope Peschalis I crowns Lotharius I as co-emperor of France
Bishop Giovanni "Minchus" ["the thin"] elected as Anti-Pope Benedict X
Three hundred nobles in the Habsburg Netherlands submit the Compromise of Nobles petition to Margaret of Parma against the Inquisition and the enforcement of the placards against heresy
Clemens Crabeels becomes bishop of 's-Hertogenbosch
Daimyo (Lord) of the Satsuma Domain in southern Kyūshū, Japan, completes his successful invasion of the Ryūkyū Kingdom in Okinawa
Second parliament ofKing James Ibegins session, no bills pass and lasts only 2 months and 2 days, thus its name the Addled Parliament
Spanish troops and feudal barons strike down people's uprising in Naples
France and Prussia form an alliance against Austria over partitioning parts of the duchies of Jülich and Berg
Adolf Frederik of Holstein-Gottorp crowns himself King of Sweden
British take Grenada, West Indies, from the French
First US Chamber of Commerce forms in NYC
High Possil Meteorite: The first recorded meteorite in Scotland falls in Possil
Isaac Quintard patents apple cider
British storm Badajoz fortress in Extremadura, Spain, held by French & Spanish
Netherlands Bank issues its 1st banknotes
First civic public park, Birkenhead Park, designed by Joseph Paxton, opens in Birkenhead, England
Federals abandon Ft Quitman, Texas
Siege of Yorktown, fought in York County and Newport News, Virginia ends (Yorktown campaign), inconclusive result (US Civil War)
Battle at Amelia Springs, Jetersville Virginia (Appomattox Campaign)
Johann Strauss Jr's opera "Die Fledermaus" premieres in Vienna
Chile declares war on Bolivia and Peru, starting the War of the Pacific
Transvaal regains independence under British suzerainty
Cleveland passes Park Act (forerunner of Metroparks)
11 strikers killed in riot at Connellsville, Penn
The Greco-Turkish War, also called "Thirty Days' War", is declared between Greece and the Ottoman Empire.
Attempted assassination of Edward Prince of Wales in Brussels, fails
Under threats from the Ottoman Turkish Government, Bulgaria is forced to arrest the leaders of the Macedonian Committee
In Glasgow, Scotland the Ibrox disaster occurs after a section of a grandstand collapses killing 25 and injuring 517
The first international rugby league match is played between England and an Other Nationalities team (Welsh & Scottish players) in Central Park, Wigan, England.
Mount Vesuvius erupts and devastates towns in the Naples province, killing more than 100 people
St Pius X encyclical "On the Mariavites or Mystic Priests of Poland"
MCC tour match v Jamaica finishes in a tie
Waldorf W Aster acquires Daily Observer
French begin Woëvre-offensive
French troops occupy Bois de Caillette
Antwerp is officially declared the host city for the Games of the 7th Olympiad to be conducted in 1920; first post WWI Olympics
Polish Army executes 35 young Jews
KOB-AM in Albuquerque NM begins radio transmissions
WDZ-AM in Decatur IL begins radio transmissions
Belgian Workers Party wins parliamentary election
Lithuania signs Litvinov-pact
England cricketers dismissed for then record 849 v West Indies in 4th Test in Kingston, Jamaica;Andy Sandhamout for 325
Dominion of Newfoundland: 10,000 rioters seize the Colonial Building leading to the end of self-government
Dutch textile strike broken by trade unions
Baseball superstarBabe Ruthagrees to do three 15-minute broadcasts a week over NBC for a fee of $39,000 for 13 weeks; $4,000 more than his NY Yankees playing contract
Croatian Farmers Party wins Yugoslavian election
Tupelo, Mississippi virtually annihilated by a tornado, 216 die
Membership of Hitler Youth becomes obligatory
San Francisco Castro & Fillmore streetcars replaced by buses
Mortsel, Belgium: Allies target Minerva car factory, used for repairing Luftwaffe planes for bombing raid; collateral damage from missed targets kills 936 civilians, Belgium's greatest loss of WWII
World War II: 145 RAF Lancasters bomb German aircraft factory in Toulouse, France
World War II: 270 inhabitants of the Greek town of Kleisoura are executed by the Germans
World War II: Dutch city of Almelo is freed by the Second Canadian Corps
WGN TV channel 9 in Chicago, IL (IND) begins broadcasting
60 year old St Anthony's Hospital in Effingham, Illinois, catches fire, killing 77 people
Fireside Theater debuts on US television.
Prague espionage trial against bishops & priests begins
Henry Wittenberg wins his 8th AAU wrestling title
WEYI TV channel 25 in Saginaw, MI (CBS) begins broadcasting
Ceylon's Mahajana Eksath Peramuna (MEP), led by S.W.R.D. Bandaranaike wins the general elections in a landslide
Ripple Rock, an underwater threat to navigation in the Seymour Narrows in Canada is destroyed in one of the largest ever non-nuclear controlled explosions
Dutch governor Platteel installs New Guinea Council
Herb Gardner's "Thousand Clowns" premieres in NYC
Mgr J Daems appointed bishop of Antwerp
St Bernard Tunnel finished-Swiss/Italians workers shake hands
Beatles receive their 1st silver disc, for "Please Please Me"
Susuga Malietoa Tanumafili II becomes chief of Western Samoa
1st driverless trains run on London Underground
Lava Lamp Day celebrated
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
WTVX TV channel 34 in Ft Pierce-Vero Beach, FL (IND) 1st broadcast
ATS II launched but fails to reach orbit
Massive anti-Vietnam War demonstrations occur in many U.S. cities
WSNS TV channel 44 in Chicago, IL (IND) begins broadcasting
Fran Phipps is 1st woman to reach North Pole
Mount Etna erupts in Sicily
US Lt William Calley sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering 22 unarmed South Vietnamese civilians in the My Lai Massacre
WNJT TV channel 52 in Trenton, New Jersey (PBS) begins broadcasting
Mets trade Ken Singleton to Expos for Rusty Staub
The regular MLB season fails to open due to a player strike for the first time in history; 86 games are lost before the labor dispute settled
NFL adopts jersey numbering system (ie QBs, 1-19)
Pioneer 11 launched to Jupiter
Soviet Soyuz 18A launch aborted short of orbit; cosmonauts return safely
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Philadelphia Flyers amass a record 2,621 penalty minutes
43-year-old St. Louis reliever Jim Kaat pitches 1 inning for the Cardinals in a season-opening, 14-3 rout of Houston; sets a MLB record for pitchers by playing in his 24th consecutive season
British Royal Navy aircraft carriers Invincible and Hermes, with escort vessels, depart Portsmouth, England for the Falkland Islands in response to the Argentine invasion
Lord Carrington, British Foreign Secretary resigns for his failure to foresee the invasion of the Falkland Islands
France throws out 47 Soviet diplomats
Record for a throw-and-return boomerang toss is set (121m)
US soldier & Turkish woman killed in West Berlin disco bombing
Fox TV network premieres "Married... with Children" and "The Tracey Ullman Show"
Poland grants legal status to trade union Solidarność (Solidarity)
NY Rangers beat NY Islanders 2-1 in 1st game of preliminary
Detroit Pistons Joe Dumaars ends NBA free throw streak of 62 games
Southeast Airlines Embracer 120 crashes in Georgia, killing 23
Space Shuttle STS 37 (Atlantis 8) launched from the Kennedy Space Center, Florida
US begins air drops to Kurd refugees in Northern Iraq
Game 2 of Mayor Challenge - NY Yankees sweep NY Mets 6-5 at Shea
Serbian troops begin besieging Sarajevo, the capital of Bosnia, which would become the longest siege in modern warfare
Several hundred-thousand abortion rights demonstrators march in Washington, D.C.
Thai General Suchinda Kraprayoon installed as Prime Minister
73,293 see the NY Yankees beat the Cleveland Indians, 9-1 in the season opener at Cleveland Stadium
A win and a loss for MLB expansion teams in their debut games; Florida Marlins beat LA Dodgers, 6-3 in Miami; the Colorado Rockies lose to Mets, 3-0 in NYC
Miami Heat beat NY Knicks ending 15 game NBA winning streak
Pages of Codex Argenteus (the Silver Bible), the oldest text in the Gothic language (5th century) stolen from Uppsala University Library, Sweden in broad daylight (recovered a month later)
John Bobbitt is put under house arrest in Las Vegas for 120 days
Galileo, 3rd Ganymede Flyby (Orbit 7)
The Arizona Diamondbacks record their first win in team history with a 3-2 victory over the San Francisco Giants in Phoenix, AZ; ends second-worst start ever (0-5) by an expansion team
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, GC at Desert Mountain: Defending champion Gil Morgan wins by 2 strokes from Tom Wargo
The Kansas City Royals become the first MLB team to begin the season 5-0 after losing 100 games the previous year, beating the Cleveland Indians, 2 - 1
The Washington Nationals lose their inaugural season opener, 8-4 to the Philadelphia Phillies; first team to represent the nation's capital since the Washington Senators left after the 1971 season

North Korea launches its controversial Kwangmyŏngsŏng-2 rocket. The satellite passed over mainland Japan, which prompted an immediate reaction from the United Nations Security Council, as well as participating states of Six-party talks
77-year old pensioner's suicide outside Greek parliament prompts further protests in Athens
A three-run homer by J.P. Arencibia off Jairo Asencio gives the Toronto Blue Jays a 7-4 win over the Cleveland Indians; 16 inning game duration a MLB Opening Day record
International internet group Anonymous hack several Chinese bureaus in opposition to censorship
Severe storms in Argentina kill 14 people
60 people are killed in Nigeria after a bus collides with an oil tanker
Baltimore outfielder Chris Davis becomes 4th player to homer in first 4 games of a MLB season; 8th-inning grand slam propels Orioles to a 9-5 win over the Twins; his RBI total of 16 a MLB record
Japan’s Nikkei 225 reaches its highest level in five years
Minnesota manager Ron Gardenhire picks up the 1,000th win of his MLB career as the Twins score a 7 - 3 victory over the Cleveland Indians
Rolling Stone Magazine retracts its "Rape on Campus" story about a gang rape at the University of Virginia after being discredited
Iceland's Prime Minister Sigmundur David Gunnlaugsson resigns after the Panama Papers leak show a conflict of interest
PayPal announces it is cancelling a $3.6 million investment in North Carolina after the state passes anti-gay legislation
San Francisco becomes the first US city to mandate paid parental leave
OKC guard Russell Westbrook becomes first player in NBA history to average a triple-double for the third consecutive season during the Thunder's 123-110 win at home over Detroit Pistons
India records over 100,000 new daily COVID cases for the first time, more than half in the state of Maharashtra, which begins a new lockdown
Italy scraps its 1914 film censorship law that could ban films on moral and religious grounds
Tropical Cyclone Seroja causes floods and landslides in southeast Indonesia and East Timor, killing at least 113 people according to local authorities
Shanghai lockdown extended to cover the whole city as COVID-19 cases increase, as China remains one of the last to employ a "zero-Covid" system
New York region hit by a 4.8 magnitude earthquake, with multiple aftershocks. One of the largest earthquakes for a century in the area.
Russian Orsk city dam bursts amid flooding on the Ural river prompting thousands to evacuate there, downstream and in border areas of Kazakhstan
Floods affecting half of Kinshasa, capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo, are reported to have killed 33 people, amid claims the climate crisis is making flooding worse
Science's $3 million Breakthrough Prize awarded to scientists who led development of weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy, Daniel Drucker, Joel Habener, Jens Juul Holst and Lotte Bjerre Knudsen
Earth's 1st contact with the extraterrestrial Vulcan species in the Star Trek universe
Battle on the Ice: Russian Prince of NovgorodAlexander Nevskydefeats the Teutonic Knights on the frozen Lake Peipus between Estonia and Russia
Scottish KingJames Ireturns to Scotland after 18 years of detention at the English court
New EnglishKing James I(James VIof Scotland) departs Edinburgh for London
British Parliament passes the Sugar Tax on the American colonies, introduced by Prime MinisterGeorge Grenville
George Washingtonexercises first presidential veto to strike down a Congressional bill to increase the number of seats for northern states in the House of Representatives
1st performance ofLudwig van Beethoven's 2nd Symphony in D, conducted by the composer, in Vienna
Battle of Maipú: Chile's independence movement, led by Bernardo O'Higgins andJosé de San Martín, wins a decisive victory over Spain, leaving 2,000 Spaniards and 1,000 Chilean patriots dead

Anne Sullivan teaches "water" to blind and deaf six-year-oldHelen Kellerby holding one of her hands under a dripping water pump and spelling out “w-a-t-e-r” in Keller’s palm. She goes on to learn how to read, write, speak and graduate from college.
Oscar Wildeloses libel case against Marquess of Queensberry, who accused him of homosexual practices
Maurice Ravel's "Pavane pour une infante defunte (Pavane for A Dead Princess)" premieres in Paris, performed by Spanish pianist Ricardo Viñes
J. M. Barrie's stage play "Alice Sit-by-the-Fire" premieres in London
British Prime MinisterHenry Campbell-Bannermanresigns
Jess Willard KOsJack Johnsonafter 26 rounds to win the heavyweight boxing title in front of 25,000 fans at Oriental Park Racetrack in Havana, Cuba
Eamon de Valera becomes President of Dail Eireann, the Irish parliament
FirestoneTire and Rubber Company starts producing inflatable tires
Yankees whip Dodgers in exhibition 16-9 butBabe Ruthcollapses in NC due to an ulcer
Alexander Gretchaninov's 5th Symphony premieres at the Academy of Music in Philadelphia with Leopold Stowkowski conducting the Philadelphia Orchestra
1st performance ofCharles Ives' 3rd Symphony
Julius &Ethel Rosenberg, atomic spies, sentenced to death
Anthony EdensucceedsWinston Churchillas Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Barbra Streisandappears on "Jack PaarShow"
NASA'sNeil Armstrongpilots the X-15 to a height of 54,600 meters
37th Academy Awards: "My Fair Lady",Rex Harrison(My Fair Lady) &Julie Andrews(Mary Poppins) win
'76erWilt Chamberlainsets NBA record of 41 rebounds
Concert by singerJames Brownat Boston Garden arena is broadcast live on public television in the aftermath of Martin Luther King's assassination
Sri Lanka's communist Janatha Vimukthi Peramuna (JVP) launches insurrection against the United Front government ofSirimavo Bandaranaike
American tennis #1Chris Evertwins her 3rd WTA Tour Championship at the Los Angeles Sports Arena; beatsMartina Navratilova6-4, 6-2
Harold Wilsonresigns asJames Callaghanbecomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Thousands gather in Tiananmen Square to commemorate former leaderZhou Enlaiand ask for more openness in government
Tom Stoppard's play "Dirty Linen and New-Found-Land" premieres in London
NY MetTom Seaver's sets record 14th NL Opening Day assignment
Kareem Abdul-Jabbarbreaks Wilt Chamberlain's all-time career scoring record of 31,419 points
Wayne Gretzkywins 7th straight NHL scoring title
Los Angeles DodgerOrel Hershiser's consecutive scoreless streak of 59 innings ends
NBC's "Late Night WithDavid Letterman" becomes 1st network TV show broadcast in Dolby Stereo
John Stocktonreaches 1000-assist mark for NBA record 3 yrs in a row
Paul Newmanwins a court victory over Julius Gold, to keep giving all profits from Newman foods to charity
Kitty Kelley publishes an unauthorized biography of former US First LadyNancy Reagan

Steve Irwin's "The Crocodile Hunter" debuts
"One Dance" single Released byDrake(Billboard Song of the Year 2016)
Pepsi ad featuringKendall Jennerpulled after criticized for trivializing demonstrations
British monarchQueen Elizabeth IImakes an address to the nation “we will meet again”, for only the 5th time in her 66-year reign
British Prime MinisterBoris Johnsonadmitted to hospital suffering from COVID-19
Ukrainian President VolodymyrZelenskyyurges UN Security Council to act against Russia, accusing Russian military of the worst war crimes since WWII, including execution, rape and torture of civilians
Saint Patrick returns to Ireland as a missionary bishop
Mayflower sails from Plymouth, Massachusetts on a return trip to England
Dutch navigator Jacob Roggeveen is the first European to discover Easter Island, also known as Rapa Nui, in the southeastern Pacific Ocean
Mount Tambora in the Dutch East Indies, has its first violent eruption after several centuries of dormancy

Mahatma Gandhiand thousands of followers reach the sea at Dandi and boil seawater to make illegal salt, marking the end of his famous 200 mile (300km) protest march against the widely hated British salt tax
Chinese steward Poon Lim is found off the coast of Brazil by a Brazilian fishing family after being adrift for 133 days following the torpedoing of the British ship SS Benlomond by a German U-boat
Two Libyans suspected of bringing down Pan Am flight 103 in 1988 are handed over for eventual trial in the Netherlands
